questions.scenario: An inbound NSG rule allows TCP 443 from `asg-app-gateway` to `asg-orders-api`. What does it allow?

options.text: New HTTPS connections from the application gateway group to the orders API group.
All traffic from the Internet to the API subnet.
Database writes from the API to Azure SQL.
Management-plane edits to the API resource.

questions.explanation: The rule names direction, protocol, port, source, and destination. It does not grant database access or management permissions.

questions.scenario: Why are priority numbers important in NSG rules?

options.text: Azure checks lower priority numbers first and stops at the first matching rule.
Higher numbers always allow more secure traffic.
Priorities decide which resource group owns the NSG.
Priority only affects the portal display order.

questions.explanation: NSG rule priority controls evaluation order. A broad earlier rule can prevent a later rule from mattering.

questions.scenario: Which fields are part of the packet decision in an NSG rule?

options.text: The Git branch that deployed the app.

questions.explanation: NSGs evaluate network traffic fields. Deployment metadata can help review but it is not a packet-match field.
